elev_above_pit.description: Calculate elevation above the nearest depression (pit). Useful for drainage analysis and identifying topographic prominence.

flow_accumulation_d8.description: Calculate flow accumulation using D8 (8-neighbor) flow direction. Standard method for identifying flow paths and stream networks.

dem.label: Input DEM. Should be hydrologically conditioned (depressions filled).

weight.label: Weight raster [optional]
weight.tooltip: If provided, flow accumulation weighted by cell values (e.g., rainfall, infiltration). Omit for uniform weighting.

output.label: Output flow accumulation raster (required in QGIS).

flow_direction_d8.description: Compute D8 flow direction (8 possible directions). Foundation for hydrological modeling and stream extraction.

dem.label: Input DEM. Should be depressions-filled for realistic flow paths.

output.label: Output flow direction raster with direction codes 1-8 (required in QGIS).

aspect.description: Calculate slope aspect (downslope direction) from DEM. Output in degrees (0–360) or compass classes (N, NE, E, etc.).

output_type.label: Output format {degrees, compass_8_direction, compass_16_direction} (default degrees). Choose based on application.
output_type.tooltip: degrees: 0–360 (0°=North, 90°=East, 180°=South, 270°=West); compass_8_direction: N,NE,E,SE,S,SW,W,NW; compass_16_direction: finer resolution

slope.description: Calculate slope gradient from DEM. Output in degrees, radians, or percent rise.

units.label: Output units {degrees, radians, percent} (default degrees). Choose based on analysis needs.
units.tooltip: degrees: 0–90 (most intuitive); radians: 0–π/2 (for calculations); percent: rise/run × 100 (common in geomorphology)

curvature.description: Calculate surface curvature (profile or plan). Identifies ridges, valleys, and inflection areas. Useful for terrain classification.

type.label: Curvature type {profile, plan, mean_curvature, gaussian_curvature} (default profile). Profile highlights valleys/ridges; plan highlights convergence/divergence.
type.tooltip: profile: curvature in downslope direction (1D); plan: curvature perpendicular to slope (1D); mean_curvature: average (2D); gaussian: product (2D)

terrain_roughness_index.description: Compute terrain roughness by measuring local elevation variability. Quantifies microtopography and surface complexity.

filter_size.label: Window size in cells (default 3, must be odd). Larger windows integrate roughness over broader areas.
filter_size.tooltip: 3: fine-scale roughness; 5–7: medium roughness; 9+: broad topographic complexity. Typical range: 3–15 cells.

terrain_position_index.description: Calculate Terrain Position Index (TPI). Classifies positions relative to neighborhood elevations (ridges, valleys, plains).

inner_radius.label: Inner radius in cells (default 3). Defines local neighborhood around each cell.
inner_radius.tooltip: Typical: 3–10 cells. Smaller values identify fine-scale features; larger values smooth to broader patterns.

outer_radius.label: Outer radius in cells (default 21 or larger). Defines context for comparison.
outer_radius.tooltip: Should be larger than inner_radius. Ratio of outer/inner defines scale. Typical: 10–50 cells.

fill_depressions.description: Fill depressions in DEM to enforce hydrological connectivity and remove spurious sinks.

dem.label: Input DEM with depressions to fill.

fix_flats.label: Fix flat areas [optional]
fix_flats.tooltip: When enabled, also resolves flat areas by introducing gentle gradients. Ensures drainage across plateaus. Recommended for DEMs with large flat regions.

viewshed.description: Calculate viewshed (visible area) from observer location(s) on terrain. Useful for siting analysis, visibility studies.

dem.label: Input DEM. Should be in same coordinate system as observer points.

observer_points.label: Observer location(s) as point vector layer. Each point is treated as viewpoint; elevation taken from DEM.

observer_height.label: Observer height in DEM units (default 1.6 meters). Height above ground for observer (e.g., eye level for person).
observer_height.tooltip: Typical: 1.6–2.0 m for person; adjustable for cameras, sensors, or analysis needs.

max_distance.label: Maximum visible distance [optional]
max_distance.tooltip: Limits viewshed computation for performance. Leave empty for unlimited visibility.

watershed.description: Delineate watersheds from flow direction raster. Identifies flow-connected regions and basin boundaries.

flow_direction.label: Flow direction raster (D8 format). Maps flow paths for watershed delineation.

pour_points.label: Pour point locations (outlet vectors) [optional]
pour_points.tooltip: If provided, delineates watershed for each point. If omitted, computes all watersheds.

output.label: Output watershed raster where each value = unique basin ID (required in QGIS).
